name: Waterfalls Restaurant
description: An eatery in a shallow river where diners can enjoy their meal mere feet from the base of an artificial waterfall.

name: Hinatuan Enchanted River
description: A magically clear, blue river that seems to spring from nowhere.

name: Asik-Asik Falls
description: This wondrous waterfall rushing down a green-covered mountain wasn't discovered until 2010.

name: Hanging Coffins of Sagada
description: Hundreds of wooden coffins dangle from cliffs and caves in the Philippines.

name: Vulcan Point
description: An island in the middle of a lake, in the middle of a volcano, in the middle of a bigger lake, in the middle of a bigger island, in an archipelago, in the Pacific Ocean.

name: Barracuda Lake
description: This curious fresh and salt water lake holds a secret that draws in deep divers from around the world.

name: Philippine Tarsier and Wildlife Sanctuary
description: A patch of Philippines forest devoted to saving the lives of an emotionally sensitive species of googly-eyed primates.

name: Chocolate Hills
description: Bohol's unusually shaped hills turn brown in the summer months.

name: The Ruins in Talisay
description: A once great mansion stands in stately ruin.

name: Abandoned Puerto Azul
description: The once-grand remains of this resort are slowly turning into the strange ruins of the future.

name: Tinago Falls
description: Hidden deep within a Filipino jungle is an incredible waterfall that plunges into a dazzling blue-green pool.

name: Puerto Princesa Subterranean River
description: Five miles of underground river link lake to ocean.

name: Sunken Cemetery
description: A cemetery and the town it served sinks beneath the sea during the volcanic birth of Mt. Vulcan.

name: Corregidor Island
description: The ruins on a deserted tropical island in the Philippines bear the scars of WWII.

name: Old Diplomat Hotel
description: This eerie abandoned building with a dark history is said to be one of the most haunted places in the Philippines.

name: Timbac Mummies of the Philippines
description: Smoked mummies housed in mountainside caves.

name: Atching Lillian Restaurant
description: In her ancestral home, one chef preserves ancient Filipino recipes and serves up traditional, home-cooked food to history-loving foodies.

name: Japanese Tunnels of Baguio
description: Some locals believe the fabled lost gold of General Yamashida is hiding somewhere beneath the city.

name: Ursula Island
description: This tiny island protects a number of rare bird species by only allowing visitors to set foot on the beach.

name: Cagsawa Ruins
description: This belfry is all that remains of the town that was buried by a violent volcano eruption.

name: Mabua Pebble Beach
description: A unique beach made of a myriad of multi-sized pebbles.

name: Bamboo Organ
description: Church organ in the Philippines constructed out of bamboo.

name: Cemetery Beach
description: Nearly 100 concrete graves lie hidden just beyond the pristine waters along the coast.

name: Lion's Head
description: Just off the road outside Baguio, a gigantic lion's head carved into a mountain greets those driving by.

name: Batad Rice Paddies
description: These tiered rice paddies were carved into the hillsides 2,000 years ago.

name: Christ of the Divine Mercy
description: A 50-foot-tall statue of Jesus where a stairway transports the faithful into his body.

name: Fort Drum (El Fraile Island)
description: A man-made island designed to look like a massive battleship.

name: Aguinid Falls
description: Calcium-rich water has turned the water and rocks of these Philippines falls slightly green.

name: Mount Banahaw
description: This volcano in the Philippines is believed to have miraculous healing powers.

name: Tangub City Christmas Symbols Festival
description: An annual festival full of illuminated replicas of iconic landmarks from around the world.

name: Clark Air Base Hospital
description: The remains of an abandoned air force hospital located on a redeveloped Air Force base.

name: Lumiang Cave
description: This burial cave houses around 100 ancient coffins, neatly stacked to form a massive wall.

name: The Sinking Bell Tower
description: It is believed that the historic tower is sinking at a rate of an inch a year, though no one is completely sure why.

name: Nagcarlan Underground Cemetery
description: The crypt under this cemetery's chapel was used as a meeting place by Filipino revolutionaries.

name: APEC Sculpture Garden
description: A melting pot of Asian-Pacific art features one symbolic sculpture from each of 20 different nations.

name: San Sebastian Church
description: A church made of steel.

name: Tinuy-An Falls
description: Sometimes known as the little Niagara Falls of the Philippines.

name: Demon in the Cemetery
description: A very peculiar and macabre statue calls Tugatog Cemetery home.

name: Cata-al World War II Museum
description: A vast personal collection of World War II artifacts unearthed over many years by a father-son duo.

name: Marikina Shoe Museum
description: Iron Butterfly's personal collection of footwear.

name: World's Largest Shoes
description: Marikina's record-breaking loafers.

name: Magellan's Cross
description: This holy site either displays a centuries-old cross or a complete fake.

name: Kaman-utek Blue Soil
description: Hues of distinctive blue to bluish-green color the slopes of these hills.

name: Fortune Island
description: This Philippine island is dotted with (fake) Grecian ruins.

name: Paco Park Cemetery
description: The cemetery was built for the Spanish colonial elite, but its most famous residents were all buried in unmarked graves.

name: Linamnam
description: Feast on contemporary Filipino cuisine in a chef’s backyard.

name: Manila Clock Tower
description: This iconic clock tower is the largest in the Philippines.

name: Santo Niño de Cebú
description: A small statue of child Jesus given by Magellan in 1521

name: Cemetery of Negativism
description: A place where you can symbolically rid yourself of negative thoughts and attitudes.

name: Arroceros Forest Park
description: Known as Manila's "last lung," this park used to house a market, garrison, and various government buildings.

name: Marcos Museum and Mausoleum
description: The late, disgraced President's embalmed remains displayed for all to see… theoretically.

name: Largest Rosary in the World
description: This massive Roman Catholic rosary is composed entirely of wood.

name: Lagusnilad (Manila City Hall Underpass)
description: Once a crime hotspot, this refurbished underpass is now home to a bookstore and a mural celebrating the history of Manila.

name: Manila Metropolitan Theater
description: This historic Art Deco building embellished with local tropical fruits had fallen into disrepair before a recent restoration.

name: Manila Film Center
description: A film center envisioned to rival Cannes ended up as a haunted place after at least 168 people were killed during the rushed construction.

name: Snake Island of Palawan
description: This unusual island has a winding sand bridge that only appears at low tide.

name: Vargas Museum
description: Philippine art and culture are on display at this university museum.

name: Golden Regalia
description: This gold sash was part of a massive collection of pre-Hispanic gold artifacts discovered in an isolated village in the Philippines.

name: El Deposito Underground
description: The largest Spanish artifact built in the Philippines and one of the oldest underground reservoirs in the world.

name: San Agustin Church
description: The first religious structure on the island of Luzon and a survivor of World War II.

name: Manila American Cemetery and Memorial
description: The largest United States military cemetery overseas.

name: Sulfur Hills of Pasil
description: This steaming, yellow-tinged landscape is filled with sulfur-encrusted volcanic vents.

name: GOMBURZA Monument
description: Dedicated to the three priests whose execution inspired author José Rizal.

name: College of Engineering Sundial
description: A series of increasingly larger sundials have grown with this school.

name: Sultan Kudarat Provincial Capitol
description: This grand building inspired by classic Islamic architecture is home to a seat of provincial government.

name: Balaw Balaw Restaurant and Art Gallery
description: This ambient art gallery and eatery housed in an old Filipino-style building serves local traditional food.

name: Ganga Caves
description: These caves are full of coffins, but we can't say who the bodies belong to.

name: Memorare Manila 1945
description: A monument dedicated in memory of the over 100,000 civilian deaths during the liberation of Manila in World War II.

name: House of Seashells
description: A house and its fence that are entirely covered with sea shells and giant clams.

name: Bellevue Theater
description: Once an art-deco theater, now a supermarket.

name: Alab Petroglyphs
description: Engravings of penises and vaginas on a boulder in the Philippines dates local tribal history back to the Bronze Age.

name: Lapu-Lapu Monument
description: This statue honors the Philippine warrior who repelled a foreign invader and prevented Ferdinand Magellan from circumnavigating the globe.

name: K-9 Cemetery
description: A memorial for military police dogs in the former U.S. air base.

name: Kiangan National Shrine
description: This memorial commemorates where a Japanese commander surrendered to Allied forces on September 3, 1945.

name: Ibaloi Fire Mummy
description: The only such mummy that remains accessible to the public.

name: Kalayaan (Freedom) Tree
description: This centuries-old tree has been a witness to some of the most important events in the history of the Philippines.

name: Baguio Stone Market
description: This market built by German prisoners of war is made of local stones.

name: Subic Spanish Gate
description: This impressive gate is the only remaining remnant of a Spanish naval station.

name: Kawa Kawa Hill
description: This "hill without a hilltop" has become a famous public pilgrimage site during Holy Week.

name: Holocaust Vault
description: A vault used to safeguard documents that allowed Jewish refugees to immigrate to the Philippines during World War II.

name: Subic Bay Jet Monument
description: An abandoned U.S. Navy aircraft is on display in the city that once held the biggest foreign U.S. military base in the world.

name: Bell Church
description: Visit a Chinese Taoist temple within the Indigenous lands of the northern Philippines.

name: Blanco Family Museum
description: Collection of paintings by the celebrated Blanco Family.

name: Bahay Nakpil-Bautista
description: A century-old ancestral home was home to some of the key figures of the Philippine Revolution.

name: Camarin na Tisa
description: A rare surviving stone building from the Spanish colonial era.

name: Fragment 22
description: This piece of the Berlin Wall symbolizes the shared German and Filipino struggles for freedom.

name: Doña Aurora Street Art
description: A series of murals cast in concrete decorate the walls where the famous artist Botong once lived.

name: Arko San Juan
description: The only remaining section of an aqueduct that used to supply water to Manila during the Spanish occupation.

name: UP Carillon Tower’s Original Bells
description: Student protesters used these bells as symbols of defiance in the early 1970s.

name: El Polvorin
description: An attack on this gunpowder depot led to the start of the Philippine Revolution.

name: Burke Building
description: The first building in the Philippines with an elevator.

name: Fantasy World
description: The abandoned medieval theme park was meant to be the "Disneyland of the Philippines."
